name: product_page_2_products_30-servings-tin

observed_strengths.observation: Nutrition facts section present — regulatory and health-conscious buyer expectation met; builds trust for wellness-positioned product
Starter Kit framing ('30-servings-tin' positioned as Original Starter Kit) — bundles ritual + product into a single SKU concept, lowering barrier for first-time buyers who want a complete setup
Consistent in-page FAQ and ingredient sections carry over from product to product — educational content infrastructure is uniform across the catalog

missing_or_weak.observation: No 'Reviews' H2 visible in PDP headings — same gap as hot-cacao-bag PDP; 60,000 5-star reviews exist in meta but aren't visibly surfaced on the Original Starter Kit PDP headings

'Buy now' links to /products/coffee-starter-kit?view=main2, not this product — same cross-link pattern as other PDPs; if the coffee-starter-kit is the hero product, this is a consistent merchandising strategy, but if unintentional it creates UX confusion
Subscribe & Save toggle not confirmed — the Original 30-serving tin is the core subscription SKU by volume; whether subscription pricing is visible and prominent on this PDP is unverified

purchase_path_notes: The Original 30-servings tin is likely the highest-revenue SKU as the flagship starter kit. Key gaps: reviews not visible in headings, subscription pricing not confirmed. The Buy Now cross-link pattern to coffee-starter-kit appears site-wide rather than PDP-specific.
